symptoms.symptom_name: Acute panic with palpitations and hyperventilation

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Panic attack with incidental reticular oral lichen planus
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Adrenergic symptoms with a clear trigger are typical of panic.

symptoms.symptom_name: Patent airway (voice, swallowing, no stridor, no angioedema)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal voice/swallow and absent stridor/angioedema strongly exclude an airway emergency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral painless non-scrapable lacy white streaks without ulcer/induration

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Panic attack with incidental reticular oral lichen planus
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilateral symmetric lacy striae are characteristic of reticular oral lichen planus.
Bilateral non-indurated lacy pattern argues against malignancy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Panic onset on noticing/googling the lesions

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Panic attack with incidental reticular oral lichen planus
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A clear trigger with prior health anxiety supports panic.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine dental/GP oral examination ± baseline photography
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirm reticular oral lichen planus and document a baseline; monitor as low malignant risk.
Not indicated with a demonstrably patent airway.
